# @architecture/generate-lifecycle-reports

Generates lifecycle governance reports showing promotion candidates, deprecated/maintenance/external packages, and per-domain lifecycle distribution.

## Package identity

```text
Name: @architecture/generate-lifecycle-reports
Version: 0.1.0
Component: generate-lifecycle-reports
Type: tool
System: architecture-governance
Domain: architecture
Bounded context: architecture-governance
Owner: architecture
```

## Lifecycle

```text
Stage: active
Role: tooling
Class: active.tooling
Catalog lifecycle: production
Visibility: internal
Support level: standard
Review cadence: quarterly
```

## Ownership

```text
Owner: architecture
Change control: owner-review
Promotion eligible: true
Semver policy: internal-traceable
```

## Responsibilities

- Read package architecture metadata from apps, packages, and tools directories
- Generate lifecycle-governance-report.json and .md under reports/lifecycle/
- List promotion-eligible, deprecated, maintenance, and external packages for governance review
- Group packages by domain and owner for ownership visibility
- Support --check (detect staleness) and --write (regenerate) modes per ADR-0011

## Non-responsibilities

- Does not validate package metadata ? use validate-package-metadata for that
- Does not generate the lifecycle summary counts ? use generate-package-inventory for that
- Does not validate lifecycle transition evidence bundles

## Public exports and usage

```text
Public exports only: true
Deep imports allowed: false
```

- Run from repository root with node tools/architecture/generate-lifecycle-reports/src/index.mjs --write
- Use --check to verify reports are up to date without writing
- Use --root <path>, --format text|json, --no-reports per ADR-0011

## Boundaries

### Allowed consumers

```text
tooling
```

### Forbidden consumers

```text
external
```


### Consumes APIs

```text

```

## Operational notes

- Reports are written to reports/lifecycle/ which is gitignored
- Self-evidence is written under reports/tooling/generate-lifecycle-reports unless --no-reports is supplied
- Run generate-package-inventory first so that upstream outputs are available
